claim: RubricLab uses a three-layer architecture: YAML rubric definitions, a runner that dispatches agents and captures traces, and a judge module that scores outputs.
actionable: Design your agent evaluation system with separate layers for rubric definition, test execution, and judging to enable scalability and repeatability.
source_title: How to Evaluate LLM Agents with RubricLab

claim: LLM-as-judge evaluation eliminates the need for hand-written assertions for every test case, making evaluation more scalable.
actionable: Use an LLM judge with structured rubrics instead of writing individual test assertions for each scenario.
source_title: How to Evaluate LLM Agents with RubricLab

claim: RubricLab stores results in a local SQLite database and provides a web UI for comparing runs without requiring a separate server.
actionable: Use RubricLab's local SQLite storage and web diff UI to manage agent evaluation results without deploying backend infrastructure.
source_title: How to Evaluate LLM Agents with RubricLab
